steps.title: Identify the cost structure
steps.content: First 2 hours: flat \$950. Each hour after the first 2: \$50/hour. For $t$ total hours ($t>2$), the number of additional hours is $t-2$, costing $50(t-2)$.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$950(t-2)+50t=1{,}150$': Multiplies the \$950 flat fee by $(t-2)$ as if it scales with time, and charges \$50 for all $t$ hours rather than only the extra hours. ✗
  • Choice B — '$950(2t)+50t=1{,}150$': Multiplies the flat fee by $2t$ — nonsensical scaling. ✗
  • Choice C — '$950+50(t-2)=1{,}150$': Flat fee \$950 plus \$50 for each of the $(t-2)$ additional hours. Verify: $950+50(t-2)=1150 \Rightarrow 50(t-2)=200 \Rightarrow t-2=4 \Rightarrow t=6$ hours. Reasonable. That matches choice C — C is correct. ✓
  • Choice D — '$950+50(2t)=1{,}150$': Charges \$50 for $2t$ hours instead of $(t-2)$ extra hours. ✗
